TextENGNew: ``Ohhhhh, Akasaka-saaaaan!!!@k@vS13/11/DS41110009. It's been far too long!!!``
``Ooishi-san!@k@vS13/13/DS41120050. You haven't changed one bit!``

``Nfu fu fu fu!@k@vS13/11/DS41110011. That's a bit harsh, don't you think?@k@vS13/11/DS41110012. I just started this apple diet recently, you know?@k@vS13/11/DS41110013. I thought I'd slimmed down a little!``
changeReason: Original misses Ooishi's complaint: 'What are you saying?' vs 'That's a bit harsh, don't you think?'; Japanese お変わりなくなんてひどいですなぁ means 'saying I haven't changed is harsh!'

TextENGNew: ``But you, Akasaka-san, you've grown so much I barely recognize you! Look how sturdy you've become!``
changeReason: New translation adds detail: 'grown so much I barely recognize you! Look how sturdy!' vs 'But just look at you!! You look great!'; Japanese 見違えるくらい逞しくなった means 'became so sturdy it's hard to recognize you'

TextENGNew: ``Well, if I don't stay in at least this kind of shape, my body won't hold up on the job.``
changeReason: New translation captures the conditional: 'if I don't stay in at least this kind of shape, my body won't hold up' vs 'I have to stay in shape'; Japanese この程度には鍛えてないと includes 'at least this much'

TextENGNew: ``Hey, Kura-chan. Is this the fellow?@k@vS13/00/honda10002. That public security officer from Tokyo who came here a few years back?``
changeReason: New translation adds 'public security officer' (公安の) from the Japanese, absent from original

TextENGNew: ``Hondaya-san, it's been a long time.@k@vS13/13/DS41120053. Thank you for taking care of me back then.``
changeReason: New translation adds 'Thank you for taking care of me back then' (その節はお世話になりました), a standard Japanese courtesy expression present in the text but omitted from original

TextENGNew: ``Well, well, haven't you grown into quite the impressive young man?@k@vS13/00/honda10004. Ahh, must be nice to be young!!``
changeReason: New translation adds the specific compliment: 'grown into quite the impressive young man' (立派に成長しちゃった), more detailed than original's vague 'look at you'

TextENGNew: ``Come now, come now, you came all this way.@k@vS13/11/DS41110016. Let's head straight to the restaurant!@k@vS13/11/DS41110017. I'm a regular there, so they'll let us in before they officially open.@k@vS13/11/DS41110018. Come on, let's go!``
``Ooishi-san, a-are you sure that's okay?@k@vS13/13/DS41120055. Aren't you still on the clock?!``
``What are you talking about?@k@vS13/11/DS41110020. I'm conducting a special training session with a mission investigator from Tokyo HQ!@k@vS13/11/DS41110021. Come on, let's get going!!``
``Ah, let me make a call first.@k@vS13/11/DS41110023. They're greenhorns compared to you, Akasaka-san,@k@vS13/11/DS41110024. but let me round up some company for us!``
``Ah, perfect timing!!@k@vS13/11/DS41110026. Kuma-chaaan!!@k@vS13/11/DS41110027. How about joining us for a night on the town tonight?``
``Ooishi-san, you really, truly haven't changed at all... hahahahaha.``
Back then, Akasaka had found Ooishi's casual manner unprofessional and off-putting,@k but years of real-world experience had completely dispelled that preconception.
Ooishi had been forged by countless experiences, and everything about who he was today came from that tempering.
changeReason: New translation significantly expands: 'forged by countless experiences' vs 'that way because of his experience'; Japanese 数々の経験に鍛え抜かれた means 'thoroughly tempered by numerous experiences'

TextENGNew: Being a detective wasn't all about squaring your shoulders and charging headlong at every problem.
changeReason: New translation captures specific detective context: 'being a detective wasn't all about squaring your shoulders' vs generic 'It's not always best to take things seriously'; Japanese specifically references 刑事 (detective) work

TextENGNew: In fact, a frank and easygoing approach often yielded better results in this world.
...In Akasaka's case, he was the one who was constantly told to relax and loosen up.
Though in Ooishi's case, he could probably stand to straighten his collar just a little.
``Come on, come on!!@k@vS13/11/DS41110029. Akasaka-san, let's go!!``
``Yes, let's be off!``
``Yeah, that's a good answer.@k@vS13/11/DS41110031. You've really developed that seasoned veteran's composure.@k@vS13/11/DS41110032. That's the spirit!``
The old Akasaka would have taken that as a slight, but now he could accept it for what it was: a genuine compliment from a senior colleague.

``You've gotten quite the tolerance, Akasaka-san.@k@vS13/11/DS41110034. I don't think I can keep up with you when it comes to drinking anymore.@k@vS13/11/DS41110035. They really do say you can't hold your liquor as well once you get old...!``

``You're too kind.@k@vS13/13/DS41120059. I'm only this strong because intelligence work doesn't mix well with being a lightweight.@k@vS13/13/DS41120060. I've got a budget set aside specifically for training sessions with my junior colleagues.``
``Japanese sake, Western liquor, Shaoxing wine!@k@vS13/13/DS41120062. Sometimes we're ducking under noren curtains, other times we're under the mirror ball at some fancy club.``
``Na ha ha ha ha!!@k@vS13/11/DS41110037. Now that's the spirit!@k@vS13/11/DS41110038. You'll have to let me come along as a coach next time!``
``By all means, please come and show them how it's done.@k@vS13/13/DS41120064. The young ones these days are always whining about how they were just born unable to drink.``
``I couldn't drink at all at first either, you know.@k@vS13/13/DS41120066. The more you drink, the stronger you get, and the more you learn to appreciate the taste!``
``Exactly, exactly!!!@k@vS13/11/DS41110040. Are you listening, Kuma-chan?``
``I-I'm sorry, sir...@k@vS14/19/213100002. But I've actually gotten a lot better, you know!``
changeReason: Significantly different: 'Yeah, I heard... I can down quite a few now!' vs 'I-I'm sorry, sir... But I've actually gotten a lot better!'; Japanese 面目ないっす means 'I'm embarrassed/sorry sir', completely missed by original

TextENGNew: ``Wa ha ha ha!!@k@vS14/00/213000002. That's for sure. When you first started working with Ooishi, you were ordering oolong tea for the toast!``

``That simply won't do!@k@vS13/13/DS41120068. A toast has deep significance.@k@vS13/13/DS41120069. Refusing to drink alcohol at the toast is practically announcing that you have a problem with the gathering.``
``That's why I make sure to set those young officers straight!@k@vS13/13/DS41120071. I'm not saying you have to chug it!@k@vS13/13/DS41120072. Just the first round, at least a medium-sized mug!!``
When Akasaka first visited Okinomiya years ago, he had been wound tight as a spring with tension.
But there was no trace of that in the Akasaka of today.
...That composure was something he had earned through years of hard-won experience.
changeReason: Old has completely different content ('Ooishi realized') from JPN which describes composure earned through hard-won experience; text appears misaligned between adjacent entries.

TextENGNew: Ooishi could sense it.
changeReason: Old contains misaligned text about Akasaka learning; JPN それを大石は感じていた simply means 'Ooishi was sensing that.' Adjacent entries appear swapped in the original translation.

TextENGNew: ``Na ha ha ha ha!!@k@vS13/11/DS41110042. Looking good, Akasaka-san!!!@k@vS13/11/DS41110043. Man, you've really become dependable!``

``To me, these past few years were just counting down to retirement. But in that same time, you've grown this strong.@k@vS13/11/DS41110045. It really drives home the point that old soldiers should step aside.``
``That's not true at all!@k@vS13/13/DS41120074. If anything, I've learned the hard way just how much experience counts in this line of work.``
``Leave the grunt work and the fieldwork to us younger officers. Veterans like you, Ooishi-san, should hold your heads high at your desks and work us to the bone!``
``You hear that, Kuma-chaaan?! Did you hear thaaaat?!!``
``Ow, ow, ow, ow, yes sir, yes sir!!!@k@vS14/19/213100004. I'll do my best!!``
changeReason: Significantly different: 'Okay, okayyyy! I heard him!!' vs 'Ow, ow, ow, ow, yes sir, yes sir!!!'; Japanese いてててて means 'ow ow ow' (physical pain) and すんません means 'sorry'

TextENGNew: ``Wa ha ha ha ha!@k@vS14/00/213000004. Ooishi, Kumagai-kun is working hard too, you know.@k@vS14/00/213000005. He's the one diligently making the rounds to cover for your laziness!``
changeReason: New translation adds dig at Ooishi: 面倒臭がりのお前さんに代わって means 'on behalf of lazy you', absent from original

TextENGNew: ``Ooishi-san is quite the handful, isn't he?@k@vS13/13/DS41120077. I only worked with him for a few days, but I can certainly sympathize with what you're going through.``
``But trust me, Ooishi-san will make you into a fine officer.@k@vS13/13/DS41120079. Believe in him and stick with him to the end.``

``Y-Yes sir! I'll do my best!!@k@vS14/19/213100006. But even if I build up my drinking, I can't do anything about mahjong!!@k@vS14/19/213100007. Ooishi-san cleans out my paycheck before I can even hand it to my wife!!``

``No, no, mahjong is all about experience too!@k@vS13/13/DS41120081. The more games you play, the stronger and more confident you get!@k@vS13/13/DS41120082. Let's play together later. I'll teach you a strategy that won't lose!``
changeReason: Original mistranslation: 'Mahjong is intellectual baggage you need!' vs 'mahjong is all about experience too!'; Japanese 麻雀も経験 means 'mahjong is also about experience'

TextENGNew: ``...Ooishi, that's a fine young man you've got there.@k@vS14/00/213000010. Reminds me a bit of you back when you were reckless and wild!``
changeReason: New translation adds characterization: 'back when you were reckless and wild' from 無茶やってた (when you were being reckless), absent from original

TextENGNew: ``Come now, no way!@k@vS13/11/DS41110048. I was never nearly that handsome.@k@vS13/11/DS41110049. Nfu fu fu!``
For those who knew Ooishi well, it was the first time they had ever seen him welcome a guest in such high spirits.
changeReason: New translation specifies: 'For those who knew Ooishi well, it was the first time' vs vague 'It was unusual'; Japanese 大石をよく知る者にとって...初めてだった is more specific

TextENGNew: Ooishi and Akasaka had only worked together for a few days, years ago.
They'd exchanged New Year's cards and the like afterward, but that was about the extent of their relationship.
Yet Ooishi had never realized just how happy it would make him to see a young officer who had once been a pale-faced rookie return as such a capable man.
changeReason: New translation adds 'pale-faced rookie' from 青白い新米 (pale-faced novice), absent from original

TextENGNew: Ooishi had always intended to stay active until the day he retired.
But seeing a junior colleague grow this dependable, he was starting to think it might not be so bad to hand over his seat and watch from the sidelines.
Both Ooishi and Akasaka found themselves enjoying their reunion far more than either had expected...
The shop owner was carrying the electric sign from out front back inside.
The chairs at all the other tables had been stacked upside down, and the proprietress was lightly mopping the floor.
Before they knew it, there were no other customers left.
The clock's hands were creeping toward three in the morning.
``This is a reunion for you guys, right, Kura-chan?@k@vS14/00/master10002. Take your time, no rush.@k@vS14/00/master10003. Just wake me up when you're leaving so I can lock up.``
``Sorry for the trouble, really.``
At this hour, there was nowhere else they could move to.
Ooishi and his companions still had plenty to talk about, so they gratefully took the owner up on his kindness.
Kumagai had lost his battle with drowsiness, his head bobbing and swaying as though rocked by waves.
The conversation had turned to Akasaka's current work.
Akasaka worked in a particularly unusual division within Public Safety.
changeReason: Different: 'specializing in Public Safety' vs 'particularly unusual division within Public Safety'; Japanese 特に普通ではない部署 means 'particularly abnormal/unusual division'

TextENGNew: His tales of battle-like days and dangerous tightrope walks had Ooishi and the others hanging on every word.
``...And actually, while I'm here, there's a girl I've been meaning to see. Rika Furude.``
changeReason: Old simplifies but preserves core meaning; new adds the nuance of 'since I came here' and wanting to see 'a girl,' closer to the Japanese phrasing.

TextENGNew: ``Rika Furude? You mean little Rika-chan, the last of the Furude family?``
changeReason: Different: 'orphaned daughter' vs 'little Rika-chan, the last of the Furude family'; Japanese 忘れ形見 means 'keepsake child/last remnant' and 梨花ちゃま is a specific honorific

TextENGNew: ``The last of...?@k@vS13/13/DS41120085. ......Does that mean her parents are...?``
changeReason: Different: 'Orphaned...?' vs 'The last of...?'; 忘れ形見 is being echoed back, meaning 'keepsake/last remnant', not 'orphaned'

TextENGNew: Rika Furude had once prophesied to Akasaka that a series of strange incidents would occur in Hinamizawa for five consecutive years.
One of those predictions, the third year if he recalled correctly, had included the deaths of her own parents.
``Yes, it was the third year of the so-called curse of Oyashiro-sama. Hmm, that would be 1981.``
``The Furude couple died under rather suspicious circumstances.@k@vS13/11/DS41110054. It was exactly three years after your visit to Hinamizawa, Akasaka-san.``
``...Three years later...?!@k@vS13/13/DS41120087. It happened in June, three years after I visited?!``
``Yes, that's right.@k@vS13/11/DS41110056. ...What is it?@k@vS13/11/DS41110057. Something bothering you?``
Akasaka squeezed his eyes shut, trying to force the alcohol from his brain, struggling to recall every detail of the girl's prophecy.
...That's right. This was the very reason he had come here...

``I first visited in 1978.@k@vS13/13/DS41120089. The following year, the dam construction foreman was killed, wasn't he?@k@vS13/13/DS41120090. ...That's right, he was the man I played mahjong with.@k@vS13/13/DS41120091. What happened to him?``
changeReason: New translation restructures to include all details: explicitly states 'The following year, the dam construction foreman was killed' and 'That was the man I played mahjong with' rather than leaving it ambiguous

TextENGNew: ``...I thought it made a big splash in the papers, but maybe that was just the local ones.@k@vS13/11/DS41110059. ...Well, the following year, he was killed in a dispute with the workers.``
The man had been brutally murdered, and then...
changeReason: Old fabricates content about Rika predicting dismemberment, not present in this line's JPN which simply states 'brutally murdered, and then...' Text appears misaligned.

TextENGNew: ``Was his body... dismembered?``
``Oh, so you already know about it.@k@vS13/11/DS41110061. ...That's exactly right.@k@vS13/11/DS41110062. His right arm still hasn't been found.@k@vS13/11/DS41110063. Poor soul...``
...The following year, on this same day in June 1980.
......Satoko's parents will be pushed off a cliff and die.
changeReason: New translation specifies: 'pushed off a cliff and die' vs 'pushed to their deaths'; Japanese 突き落とされて死にます specifically mentions being pushed off (a cliff)

TextENGNew: ...Or perhaps it should be called an accident.
...An unfortunate accident.
``L-Let me continue confirming.@k@vS13/13/DS41120094. The year after that.@k@vS13/13/DS41120095. The year after the foreman's murder.``
changeReason: Different: 'I'll continue' vs 'L-Let me continue confirming'; Japanese つ、続けて確認します shows stuttering and 'confirming' specifically

TextENGNew: ``......If I recall, ...um, ...that's right.@k@vS13/13/DS41120097. Satoko-san, I believe she's a young girl.@k@vS13/13/DS41120098. Did her parents die in a fall?``

``My, you are well-informed.@k@vS13/11/DS41110065. That's correct.@k@vS13/11/DS41110066. The Houjou couple, who had supported the dam, fell from an observation platform while on vacation.@k@vS13/11/DS41110067. Whether it was an accident or foul play has never been determined.``
And then the following year, on this same day in June 1981.
...My parents will be killed.
``...And then, ...the year after that...``
``Yes.@k@vS13/11/DS41110069. Rika Furude's parents died.@k@vS13/11/DS41110070. ...But that case never sat right with me either.``
changeReason: Different: 'none of those incidents make sense' vs 'that case never sat right with me either'; Japanese 今ひとつ腑に落ちない means 'doesn't quite sit right with me'

TextENGNew: ``You sure know a lot about the curse of Oyashiro-sama, young man.@k@vS14/00/213000012. These incidents were kept quiet due to pressure from the locals,@k@vS14/00/213000013. so I'm surprised you know so much about them from all the way in Tokyo.``

changeReason: Different: 'I can't believe this' vs 'What in the world'; Japanese 何てことだ means 'what in the world / what a situation'

TextENGNew: ``But even you can't know about the year after that.@k@vS14/00/213000015. Starting with the fourth year, the investigation was classified as confidential.``
``...No, ......I think I do know.``
And then the year after that, on this same day in June 1982.
Satoko's cruel aunt will be killed. Her skull will be bashed in.
changeReason: Different: 'mean aunt' vs 'cruel aunt'; but more importantly 'will die' vs 'will be killed'; Japanese 頭を割られて死にます means 'will be killed (head smashed)', new version clarifies it's murder

TextENGNew: ``The year after that would be last year.@k@vS13/13/DS41120103. ...Satoko-san's aunt, was she bludgeoned to death?``
``Th-...That's right, bull's-eye.@k@vS13/11/DS41110072. Akasaka-san, how do you know all of this?``
``......I believe... I also know who will be killed the year after that.``
``Wh-What are you saying, young man...?@k@vS14/00/213000017. The year after last year is... this year...``
...I will be killed.
``June 1983.@k@vS13/13/DS41120106. In other words, this month.@k@vS13/13/DS41120107. ...Rika Furude will be killed.``
Ooishi and the others were struck speechless.
Akasaka had laid out four consecutive mysterious deaths.
Although he had been stationed far away for a long time, he belonged to a highly specialized unit within Public Safety.
Even with the investigations classified as confidential, it would have been possible for him to learn of them.
...That was why it wasn't terribly surprising that Akasaka, despite years away from Hinamizawa, could recount the string of mysterious deaths one after another.
But it was the look of stunned disbelief on Akasaka's own face as his words proved true, one after another,
changeReason: Significantly restructured: original simply says 'he also named the next victim'; new version describes Akasaka's own shocked face as his predictions proved true, matching the Japanese which describes his 呆然とした表情 (stunned expression)

TextENGNew: and his naming of this year's predicted victim that sobered them all up completely...
changeReason: Different: original says simply 'had sobered up entirely'; new version connects the sobering to seeing Akasaka name this year's victim, matching the Japanese structure

TextENGNew: ``Akasaka-san, ......it's gotten very late.@k@vS13/11/DS41110074. We're all exhausted, so let's be direct about this.@k@vS13/11/DS41110075. ...What exactly does all of this mean?``
``Right.@k@vS13/13/DS41120109. Everything I just told you is what she told me when I visited Hinamizawa in June of 1978.``
``.........What did you say...?``
Ooishi wanted to say 'That's absurd,'@k but the shock left his jaw hanging open, and no further words would come.
changeReason: Different: 'couldn't believe what he was hearing. He was left speechless' vs 'wanted to say That's absurd, but the shock left his jaw hanging open'; Japanese describes the specific interrupted reaction

TextENGNew: ``I understand how hard this is to believe.@k@vS13/13/DS41120111. When she told me back then, I thought it was nothing more than a little girl's whimsical game.@k@vS13/13/DS41120112. ...But she told me that in the fifth year, she herself would be killed.``
``That was clearly a plea for help, I think.``
changeReason: Different: 'she asked me to help her, I think' vs 'That was clearly a plea for help, I think'; Japanese 明白なメッセージ means 'clear message'

TextENGNew: ``...The main reason I came to Hinamizawa this time was to find her and confirm whether she still needs my help.``
``...Akasaka-san, I'm retiring this year.@k@vS13/11/DS41110078. I want to avenge the foreman who was killed in the first year, no matter what, before my time is up.``
changeReason: Different: 'take revenge on the people who killed the manager' vs 'avenge the foreman who was killed'; おやっさんの仇 means 'friend/boss's revenge'; 'manager' vs 'foreman' is also different

TextENGNew: ``That's why I'll latch onto any lead I can find, no matter how small or dubious.``
``...So let me just confirm this.@k@vS13/11/DS41110081. What you just told us isn't a joke, is it?@k@vS13/11/DS41110082. If it is, tell me now...``

``I'm telling you the truth.@k@vS13/13/DS41120116. Rika Furude told me all of this in June 1978.@k@vS13/13/DS41120117. ...But at the time, I didn't think it was important, so I never told you, Ooishi-san.@k@vS13/13/DS41120118. I'm deeply sorry for that.``

``N-No, that's understandable.@k@vS14/00/213000019. Even if someone told me something like that out of the blue, I couldn't have taken it seriously.@k@vS14/00/213000020. It's only because four years have passed that we can believe such a story now.``
changeReason: Different: 'you didn't know' vs 'that's understandable'; Japanese 無理もない means 'it's understandable/can't be helped'

TextENGNew: Ooishi must have harbored the bitter thought that if only Akasaka had shared that story with him, he might have been able to prevent his friend's death.
...But what the old forensics man said was right.
Even if he had been told back then, Ooishi wouldn't have believed it.
And in the end, his friend would have died all the same, leaving him with nothing but the regret of having failed to act.

``......You're right.@k@vS13/11/DS41110084. No, I should be the one apologizing.@k@vS13/11/DS41110085. ......Still, ...Rika Furude.@k@vS13/11/DS41110086. ...Why would Rika Furude...?@k@vS13/11/DS41110087. ......Hmmmm...``
``I don't know much about the current state of affairs in Hinamizawa either.``

``I did read in the papers at the time that the dam project had been frozen indefinitely.@k@vS13/13/DS41120121. So I assumed that with the dam conflict over, the village had returned to a peaceful life.``
``Mm. The dam conflict is certainly over.@k@vS14/00/213000022. ...But starting the following year, these mysterious deaths, locally known as the curse of Oyashiro-sama, began occurring every year.``
``...And the details are exactly as you just described.``

``......It seems I've been entrusted with something of tremendous importance.@k@vS13/13/DS41120123. ...Ooishi-san, let me ask you directly.@k@vS13/13/DS41120124. ...Do you think it's plausible that Rika Furude could be killed this year?``

``...The Furude couple had been looked down upon for their passive stance during the dam conflict.@k@vS13/11/DS41110089. That's commonly believed to be why the curse fell upon them in the third year.@k@vS13/11/DS41110090. ...Following that logic, ......Rika Furude's death.........``
``...It's possible, isn't it?``
Ooishi said nothing, and slowly nodded.
``......I'm going to try contacting Rika Furude tomorrow.@k@vS13/13/DS41120127. It seems the only thing to do is hear it directly from her.``
``Akasaka-san, could I come with you?@k@vS13/11/DS41110092. ...This matter concerning Rika Furude sounds like it could be extremely important for me as well.``
``...My my... ...you've brought us quite the extraordinary gift, young man...``
``I have to say, things are getting interesting.@k@vS14/00/213000026. I never thought I'd live to see these mystifying cases actually get solved.``

``However, I do have questions of my own.@k@vS13/13/DS41120129. Why did she confide something this important to me alone?@k@vS13/13/DS41120130. If she wanted help, the logical choice would be the local police, not a visiting officer from Tokyo.``
``.........Hmm.@k@vS13/11/DS41110094. ...Most likely, she couldn't fully trust the local police.``
changeReason: Different: 'I bet she doesn't have faith in the local police' vs 'she couldn't fully trust the local police'; 信用しきれない means 'couldn't fully trust', more nuanced

TextENGNew: ``I'm a rough-around-the-edges sort myself.@k@vS13/11/DS41110096. Rika-san may not have trusted me.@k@vS13/11/DS41110097. ...Besides, there are more than a few people at the Okinomiya station with ties to the S-family.``
``In other words, I was the most trustworthy option, precisely because I came from Tokyo...?``
changeReason: Different framing: 'she trusted me more than anybody' (positive) vs 'I was the most trustworthy option, precisely because I came from Tokyo...?' (circumstantial); Japanese frames it as a question

TextENGNew: ``It's galling to admit, but that's probably the case.``
changeReason: Different: 'Yeah, I guess' (casual) vs 'It's galling to admit, but that's probably the case' (reluctant); Japanese 悔しいですが means 'it's frustrating/galling but'

TextENGNew: ``...If that's so, Ooishi-san.@k@vS13/13/DS41120133. Please keep this matter strictly between us.``
``Of course.@k@vS13/11/DS41110100. ...In any case, let's go to Hinamizawa tomorrow and speak with Rika-san directly.@k@vS13/11/DS41110101. We can decide what to do from there.``
``......I can hardly believe it.@k@vS13/05/DS41050060. ......But... that is the truth, isn't it?``
In the quiet depths of the night, only the chirping of insects and Satoko's snoring could be heard.
Rika sat by her favorite spot at the window.
``...That's right.@k@vS13/12/DS41070059. The one who killed you, Rika... was Takano.@k@vS13/12/DS41070060. You and I both saw it clearly.``
``You tried so hard to burn Takano's face into your memory...@k@vS13/12/DS41070062. Even as she sliced your stomach open, you gritted your teeth and kept your eyes locked on her face...``

``............What an awful way to die.@k@vS13/05/DS41050062. ...I suppose I should be grateful I never retained that memory.@k@vS13/05/DS41050063. ......But somehow, I can picture it.@k@vS13/05/DS41050064. That's exactly the kind of killing Takano would favor.``
``...Au au au au...``
``But I don't understand.@k@vS13/05/DS41050066. ......Why would Takano kill me?@k@vS13/05/DS41050067. I'm the queen carrier. If I die, it would trigger a catastrophe, wouldn't it?``
``Isn't that the entire reason the Irie Institute and the Mountain Dogs protect me?@k@vS13/05/DS41050069. So why would Takano, who sits at the very top, have me killed?``

``Sorry, that's a pointless question.@k@vS13/05/DS41050071. ...Takano killed me.@k@vS13/05/DS41050072. That's the truth, and her motive is beside the point.@k@vS13/05/DS41050073. ...But... even so.@k@vS13/05/DS41050074. ......Of all people, why Takano...?``
Even as she said the motive didn't matter, the question clawed its way out of her mouth almost immediately.
changeReason: Different: 'she went back to the same question' vs 'the question clawed its way out of her mouth almost immediately'; different imagery for the compulsion

TextENGNew: Takano's protection of the queen carrier wasn't solely about preventing the catastrophe of a village-wide outbreak.
changeReason: Different scope: 'wasn't because she wanted to prevent the rest of Hinamizawa from developing the syndrome' vs 'wasn't solely about preventing'; Japanese だけではない means 'not just/solely', not complete negation

TextENGNew: ...Rika was an invaluable specimen for her obsessive research.
changeReason: New translation adds 'obsessive' (偏執的な = obsessive) and 'specimen' (サンプル); original's 'subject of her research' is less specific

TextENGNew: So why would she, with her own hands, kill the queen carrier who was more precious to her research than anything?
``Is Takano my only enemy?``
``...Au au...@k@vS13/12/DS41070066. The Mountain Dogs were enemies too.@k@vS13/12/DS41070067. I'm not sure whether Irie was an enemy or not... but I don't think he was.``
changeReason: Tense change: 'The Mountain Dogs are after you too' (present) vs 'The Mountain Dogs were enemies too' (past); Japanese 敵でした uses past tense

TextENGNew: ``Takano and the Mountain Dogs are my enemies...?@k@vS13/05/DS41050077. ......You can't be serious. How are a couple of girls supposed to fight against that?@k@vS13/05/DS41050078. It's impossible.``
changeReason: Different: 'How can we possibly win?' vs 'How are a couple of girls supposed to fight against...?'; Japanese 小娘 means 'young girls', adding the gender/age vulnerability aspect

TextENGNew: ``...Unless some kind of miracle happens, there's no way we can win against people like them.@k@vS13/05/DS41050080. And Watanagashi is this coming Sunday.@k@vS13/05/DS41050081. I'll be killed on the 22nd.@k@vS13/05/DS41050082. That's about one week from now, isn't it?``

``How are we supposed to fight back in that little time?@k@vS13/05/DS41050084. Should we ambush Takano with a metal bat in a dark alley?@k@vS13/05/DS41050085. That's meaningless. Takano is just one piece of the whole operation.``
``Even if we took Takano down, the Mountain Dogs would surround us and that would be the end of it!@k@vS13/05/DS41050087. What do we do? What can we possibly do?!``
``I-...I don't know either...!@k@vS13/12/DS41070069. But, but... but...@k@vS13/12/DS41070070. ......Up until now, no matter how many times we repeated this, we didn't even know who our enemy was.``
changeReason: Different: 'we didn't even know our enemy before now' vs 'no matter how many times we repeated, we couldn't even learn our enemy's name'; Japanese 何度繰り返しても refers to repeated time loops

TextENGNew: ``...This time... we finally have that answer.@k@vS13/12/DS41070072. There must be... something we can do...!``
It was the first time Hanyuu had ever pushed back this strongly.
changeReason: Different: 'first time in a long time that Hanyuu had spoken so positively' vs 'first time Hanyuu had ever pushed back this strongly'; Japanese 強く言い返す means 'strongly talked back/pushed back'

TextENGNew: Rika was caught off guard for a moment, and then realized she had been losing her composure. She took a deep breath and reset her racing thoughts.
...The biggest reason for her turmoil was the revelation that the culprit was Takano.
changeReason: Different: 'still confused' vs 'biggest reason for her turmoil was the revelation'; Japanese 最大の理由 means 'biggest/greatest reason'

TextENGNew: Setting aside Takano's personal quirks, Rika had always believed her to be a dependable ally.
changeReason: New translation adds 'setting aside Takano's personal quirks' (個人的な性格はともかく), present in Japanese but absent from original

TextENGNew: She could think of many reasons why Takano would want to keep her safe.
...But she could not think of a single reason why Takano would want her dead.
Still, Takano was the type to entertain deeply unsettling ideas.
......There was no guarantee that some reasoning far beyond common sense couldn't serve as her motive.
``...Then that means... if Tomitake is being killed...@k@vS13/05/DS41050089. ...does that make him... an ally?``
``I... I think so.@k@vS13/12/DS41070074. ...That's it, Rika.@k@vS13/12/DS41070075. You should consult Tomitake!``
......The reason Takano commanded the Mountain Dogs was because of the organization known as 'Tokyo.'
Did that mean Rika could only seek help from someone connected to 'Tokyo'?
...If so, then Tomitake was the only person she could confide in about this.
That said, Irie was surely worth consulting as well.
But then...... that was precisely why she needed to know.
She needed to know the motive behind why Takano had to kill her!
Unless she could explain that, neither of them would give her story the time of day.
On top of everything, Tomitake was hopelessly in love with Takano.
He wouldn't suspect her over anything short of ironclad proof...
Irie seemed to keep a certain distance from Takano.
He might be more inclined to believe the story than Tomitake.
......But Irie was just a doctor, largely ignorant of 'Tokyo' and its workings.
Whether he could actually be of any use was doubtful.
Go to the police?
Don't be absurd. That was absolutely out of the question.
There was no way they would believe her.
If she called to say that a shadowy organization called 'Tokyo' was trying to kill her, they would question her sanity without a second thought...!
Then what could she do? How could she save herself?
The most dangerous people in all of Hinamizawa were coming to kill her.
How could she run?
How could she fight?
What could she do, what could she possibly do...?!
...If she had the same memory Hanyuu described, of Takano killing her, she wouldn't be in such turmoil.
After all, seeing is believing.
...But she didn't remember.

Because she only had Hanyuu's word for it, the truth she needed to accept, that Takano and her forces were the enemy,@k kept threatening to collapse into escapism, dismissing it all as Hanyuu's delusion......
``...C-...Calm down, Rika.@k@vS13/12/DS41070077. We learned so much in the previous world...!@k@vS13/12/DS41070078. Don't you remember what happened in the world before this one?``
``...The previous world... you mean... the one where Rena poured gasoline all over the school and barricaded herself inside...?``

``No, not that one...!@k@vS13/12/DS41070080. There was another world after that...!@k@vS13/12/DS41070081. You really don't remember...?!@k@vS13/12/DS41070082. In that world, he came back.@k@vS13/12/DS41070083. Teppei Houjou!``

``......The worst kind of world.@k@vS13/05/DS41050092. No matter what you do, the counseling center takes a wait-and-see approach, and Satoko breaks down within days.@k@vS13/05/DS41050093. Let me guess, one of our friends developed the syndrome and attacked Teppei?``

``No, no, no!!@k@vS13/12/DS41070085. In that world, everyone joined forces, petitioned the counseling center, and properly saved Satoko!@k@vS13/12/DS41070086. In that world, Keiichi taught you so much, Rika!``
``Miracles won't happen if you don't believe in them.@k@vS13/12/DS41070088. And if everyone believes, a miracle will definitely occur!!``

``...That certainly sounds like something Keiichi would say.@k@vS13/05/DS41050095. ...But that's remarkable.@k@vS13/05/DS41050096. To actually overcome the world where Teppei returns... something we could never manage even once before...``
``Do you know how Keiichi and the others pulled off that miracle?!@k@vS13/12/DS41070090. They talked to everyone! They asked for help!``
``Instead of assuming it was hopeless, instead of agonizing alone, they consulted everyone around them...!!@k@vS13/12/DS41070092. That's how the miracle happened...!!``

``Everyone, you say?@k@vS13/05/DS41050098. ...You want me to talk to Keiichi and the others about Takano?@k@vS13/05/DS41050099. You think they'd believe me if I told them a shadowy organization called Tokyo is trying to kill me...?``
``They will believe you!!@k@vS13/12/DS41070094. They believed you in the last world!!!@k@vS13/12/DS41070095. Why won't you believe what I'm telling you, Rika...?!``
``...I'm sorry.@k@vS13/05/DS41050101. It's not that I don't believe you.@k@vS13/05/DS41050102. ...You saw it with your own eyes, so it has to be the truth.``
``............I never realized how frustrating it could be... to feel such a gulf between us just because my own memories didn't carry over...``

``In any case, the two of us worrying alone won't solve anything.@k@vS13/12/DS41070097. By ourselves, we're far too powerless...!@k@vS13/12/DS41070098. But we have a weapon.@k@vS13/12/DS41070099. That weapon is the truth!``
``We don't know her motive... but we know the culprit is Takano!!@k@vS13/12/DS41070101. That makes this the most advantageous starting position we've ever had across all our worlds...!!``
``...And yet why... why at the very end of everything... am I the only one whose memories don't connect...?!``
Hanyuu ground her teeth in sheer frustration.
...She had just witnessed the 'answer' firsthand, so she couldn't fathom why Rika, sitting there bewildered and oblivious to the true solution, didn't share those memories.
If you had seen it with your own eyes, you could believe it readily, even without understanding the motive.
But not having seen it yourself, not having that certainty... was so much harder to swallow...
Why were Hanyuu's memories intact while hers were severed...?!
Did it mean their long journey was drawing to its close?
Or perhaps... the infinitely repeating journey had already ended for her... and this was the very last trip of all...?

``...............Wheeeeew...@k@vS13/05/DS41050105. I thought this was a strange world from the start, what with everyone being able to see you and you even transferring into our school... but it seems this world is truly extraordinary.``
``...There's nothing strange about it at all.@k@vS13/12/DS41070104. ...It's simply the final world.``

``...I came to this final world so that I could stand alongside everyone and fight together.@k@vS13/12/DS41070106. ...I'm not a bystander here.@k@vS13/12/DS41070107. I'm your ally, Rika. Everyone's ally.``
``.........Come to think of it, you're right.@k@vS13/05/DS41050108. ...You've given up being a bystander.``

``......I refuse to be afraid of getting hurt and stay off the stage any longer.@k@vS13/12/DS41070109. I'll face that fear and believe, together with everyone.@k@vS13/12/DS41070110. That's how we'll make a miracle happen...!``

``This is a game world with June 1983 on the line.@k@vS13/12/DS41070112. The combatants are you, Rika, and Takano.@k@vS13/12/DS41070113. If you can't defeat your enemy before the deadline, you automatically lose.@k@vS13/12/DS41070114. Takano has many powerful pieces at her disposal, like the Mountain Dogs.``

``Gathering just a few pieces won't be nearly enough to stand against her.@k@vS13/12/DS41070116. So unless you reach out to every possible person for help and assemble a force that can match hers, the game won't even begin...!!``
``We've been learning the rules of this game board through countless repetitions.@k@vS13/12/DS41070118. It was like playing a game with no instruction manual.``
``But we've studied, we've learned how to fight, and now we even know the identity of the enemy's pieces from the very start, an unprecedented advantage...!``
``Rika, this is no time to sit around in a daze!!``

``One week until Watanagashi!@k@vS13/12/DS41070122. In the previous world we had two weeks to work with, but this time we only have one!@k@vS13/12/DS41070123. In other words, this truly is the final world!!!``
``......All right.``

``...Rather than lamenting the misfortune of what I can't remember, I should be grateful for the fortune that you do remember. If you had forgotten too, this final world would have ended while we floundered about in confusion.``
``...Besides, if I approach Keiichi and the others the right way, they might come up with some brilliant ideas.``
``...After all, Keiichi is the man who, in a world where the school was all but certain to explode, fought Rena head-on and brought her back to her senses. He pulled off a genuine miracle.``

``Exactly!@k@vS13/12/DS41070125. Let's talk to everyone we can.@k@vS13/12/DS41070126. The two of us alone are far too weak.@k@vS13/12/DS41070127. Let's gather our allies and figure out what to do...!@k@vS13/12/DS41070128. Even the road to a miracle begins with a single step!!``
``And we have to do all of that in just one week.``
Had Hanyuu ever encouraged her this passionately before?
She had always acted like a mere bystander.
...Her stance had been to expect nothing, to avoid the pain of hoping and failing.
And yet here was that same Hanyuu, so fervently, so proactively trying to intervene in this world.
......According to Hanyuu, there had been another world after the one where Rena took over the school.
......In that world, it seemed Keiichi had instilled in her a burning conviction.
...There was nothing to lose by trying.
...She would start by consulting those she thought she could trust.
Whether she struggled or did nothing, the final day was fast approaching.
In that case, she would put up the most spirited fight she possibly could.
She would never again confuse resignation with enlightenment, playing the detached witch sipping wine as the end drew near. There was nothing admirable about that...!!
``Hello.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1002. It has been quite some time.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1003. This is Nomura.``

``......Yes, thank you for the detailed materials the other day.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1005. Thanks to your thorough documentation, the officials at the Ministry of Health and Welfare now fully understand the danger of Hinamizawa Syndrome.``

``......Fu fu fu, that's entirely the fruit of your research and the late Dr. Takano's work.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1007. I've merely ensured that legitimate research receives its rightful recognition.``

``............Of course.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1009. ...I'll see to it that your materials eventually reach the Prime Minister's hands as well.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1010. The Prime Minister is the leader of this nation, after all.``

``I shall have the most powerful person in this country read the papers you and your grandfather wrote,@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1012. and I will ensure he understands what extraordinary research this is and how exceptional Hinamizawa Syndrome truly is.``
``And I will make the current board of directors, who neglected this research and so cruelly trampled upon your and your grandfather's papers, realize just how foolish they have been.``

``............Yes.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1015. Fu fu fu, I have wonderful news for you.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1016. After the terminal operation concludes, the site will be quarantined and sealed off, and a separate JSDF division will take over the research.``
``You will be welcomed there, free from factional politics and budget anxieties, able to devote yourself to your research to your heart's content.``

``............Fu fu fu, our client wishes to reward your devoted efforts, and that is simply the expression of his generosity.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1019. You will be given the position of director, and this time you can truly immerse yourself in research without restraint.``
``Fu fu fu, isn't that simply wonderful?``
``And with your research on Hinamizawa Syndrome serving as a stepping stone, you will reach the true stage that the late Dr. Takano sought.``
``You will advance your research into the parasitic entities that influence human thought, and accomplish a feat that overturns the very foundations of human knowledge.``
``......No, this isn't flattery in the least.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1024. This is our client's honest assessment of you.``
``.........I see.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1026. I'm sorry to have kept you so long.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1027. Well then, I'll be going.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1028. I'll contact you again soon.``
The woman placed the car phone's handset back in its cradle, took a breath,@k then immediately picked it up again and dialed a different number.

``Extension 1001, please.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1030. ...Yes, tell them it's Watanabe.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1031. ...............Watanabe speaking.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1032. Yes, the matter from the other day has been concluded without incident.``

``............Yes.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1034. Major Takano's materials are superb.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1035. I also brought along staff from the national research institute, so the suits swallowed it whole without question.``

``.........Fu fu fu, yes, quite.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1037. No problems on that front either.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1038. After the 20th, most cabinet ministers from rival factions will be out of Tokyo, either on overseas trips or retreats.``
``......Yes.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1040. A few uncertainties remain, but by June 23rd, every moat around the Prime Minister's residence should be completely filled.``

``............Yes.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1042. Therefore, the field operation will be executed on the night of June 22nd, with an emergency authorization request submitted to the Prime Minister on the 23rd.``
``............Yes. Every advisor available to support the Prime Minister that day will be our people.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1044. He won't harbor even a shadow of doubt.``
``.........Yes.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1046. Without question.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1047. ...The Prime Minister will sign Emergency Manual No. 34.``
``...Afterward, the investigation into Alphabet's accountability will begin, and the Okuno-Chiba faction along with the old Koizumi faction will lose a great deal of influence.``
``......Yes.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1050. Exactly.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1051. Imamura-sensei's restoration is merely a matter of time.``
``......Indeed.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1053. The Lowell Incident was a terrible blow, but we all believed from the start that he was innocent.``

``Yes... even if it goes to trial, the prosecution won't have a leg to stand on.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1055. The leak of Lowell's classified information was a trap engineered by a certain foreign agency to have him expelled from the Foreign Ministry, after all.``

``......Yes, exactly.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1057. They may have thought they had outmaneuvered us at the time, but this round is unquestionably our victory.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1058. Fu fu fu fu fu fu.``
``......Yes, understood.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1060. ...Then I look forward to seeing you in Macau next week.``
``...Fu fu fu, yes, I do love casinos.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1062. ......Yes, well then, I'll take my leave.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1063. I'll be in touch again soon.``
The woman set the car phone's handset down and let out a breath.@k After a self-satisfied smirk at how smoothly her work was progressing, she picked up the receiver once more.

``The Chief Cabinet Secretary's office, please.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1065. ...Yes, tell them it's Maizawa.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1066. ...............@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1067. .........Yes, it's me.@k@vS13/00/VT4A_nomura1068. Chief Secretary Okuno.``
